introSection: Switchback is a wooden roller coaster currently standing but not operating at ZDT's Amusement Park in Seguin, Texas, USA. It is the first, and currently only, modern wooden shuttle roller coaster.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
The ride was announced on November 18, 2014 at the IAAPA Expo in Orlando, Florida, USA. It opened to the public on October 17, 2015.

The ride was listed for sale by The Gravity Group at the 2024 IAAPA Expo in Orlando, Florida, USA.

On July 13, 2025, ZDT's Amusement Park announced that the park would permanently close on August 17, 2025.

On July 14, International Rides Management announced that it would be assisting the park in selling its attractions, including Switchback.

Switchback closed with the park on August 17.

sections.title: Design
sections.content:
While Switchback has of track, the train travels over  of track per cycle. The ride has a spike with a maximum angle of 87 degrees. There are nine airtime points and a maximum bank angle of 104 degrees.

Switchback makes two passes through a building, which was formerly a small grocery store.

sections.title: Ride experience
sections.content: After rolling out of the station, the train makes a right turn and climbs the chain lift hill, followed by a drop. Riders go past a tunnel, which was a former small grocery store and a 104 degree over-banked curve. The train goes through a second tunnel and an airtime hill. Following a left turn, riders ascend a vertical rollback. The train completes the circuit in reverse, before slowing down on the brakes after the drop. Riders move through a switch track, followed by a left turn that returns to the station.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: The front car of Switchback was on show at Gravity Group's booth at the 2014 IAAPA Expo in Orlando, Florida, USA. The trains have a locomotive theme.

fullText:
Switchback is a wooden roller coaster currently standing but not operating at ZDT's Amusement Park in Seguin, Texas, USA. It is the first, and currently only, modern wooden shuttle roller coaster.

The ride was announced on November 18, 2014 at the IAAPA Expo in Orlando, Florida, USA. It opened to the public on October 17, 2015.

The ride was listed for sale by The Gravity Group at the 2024 IAAPA Expo in Orlando, Florida, USA.

On July 13, 2025, ZDT's Amusement Park announced that the park would permanently close on August 17, 2025.

On July 14, International Rides Management announced that it would be assisting the park in selling its attractions, including Switchback.

Switchback closed with the park on August 17.

While Switchback has of track, the train travels over  of track per cycle. The ride has a spike with a maximum angle of 87 degrees. There are nine airtime points and a maximum bank angle of 104 degrees.

Switchback makes two passes through a building, which was formerly a small grocery store.

After rolling out of the station, the train makes a right turn and climbs the chain lift hill, followed by a drop. Riders go past a tunnel, which was a former small grocery store and a 104 degree over-banked curve. The train goes through a second tunnel and an airtime hill. Following a left turn, riders ascend a vertical rollback. The train completes the circuit in reverse, before slowing down on the brakes after the drop. Riders move through a switch track, followed by a left turn that returns to the station.

The front car of Switchback was on show at Gravity Group's booth at the 2014 IAAPA Expo in Orlando, Florida, USA. The trains have a locomotive theme.
